symptoms.symptom_name: Acute localised burning pain of the forearm immediately following a boiling-water scald

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Preserved, prominent pain with a clear thermal mechanism is typical of an epidermal burn where nerve endings are exposed but intact.
Scalds can be partial-thickness; pain alone does not discriminate depth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Uniform bright erythema, dry surface, no blisters/bullae

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Dry, non-blistered erythema is characteristic of an epidermal-only burn.
Absence of blisters and a dry (non-weeping) bed argues strongly against a partial-thickness injury.

symptoms.symptom_name: No blisters or bullae present (pertinent negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Blistering is a key marker of partial-thickness depth; its documented absence is needed to support a superficial classification.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pain provoked by ambient air currents across the skin

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Air-provoked pain reflects exposed but intact hypersensitive epidermal nerve endings — typical of superficial burns.

symptoms.symptom_name: Burn area ~10 x 8 cm (approximately 1% TBSA) on a non-critical site (volar forearm)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Small TBSA on a non-critical site supports outpatient/self-care management rather than referral.

symptoms.symptom_name: Brisk complete blanching on light pressure with rapid capillary refill and intact light-touch sensation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Brisk blanching, rapid refill and preserved sensation indicate intact dermal perfusion and nerve supply — clinician-confirmed features of a superficial burn; a patient can only crudely approximate the blanch test over chat.
Deeper burns show sluggish/absent blanching and altered sensation; their absence argues against partial thickness.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mild tachycardia (HR ~98) consistent with acute pain; otherwise normal vital signs

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pain-related mild tachycardia; non-discriminating and not obtainable in a chat consult without a device.

symptoms.symptom_name: No facial/airway involvement, no inhalation injury, no special-site or circumferential burn (pertinent negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of airway/special-site involvement keeps this an isolated minor burn and supports remote self-care management.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Dry non-blistered erythema + documented absence of blisters + brisk complete blanching with intact sensation together make an epidermal-only burn highly likely and a partial-thickness burn unlikely — supporting conservative self-care with safety-netting.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: No role in a ~1% superficial burn — over-investigation. Recommending routine bloods here is inappropriate.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person visual burn depth review by GP
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Appropriate ONLY as a conditional/routine review if the burn is not settling by ~3 days or if red-flag features develop — not required up front for a clearly superficial injury.
